reason: Sub-definition 'a Christian' is the noun sense; this entry is pos:adj. The adjective has one clean meaning. Clear the stale noun sub-definition.

Sub-definition 'critique, criticism (critical appraisal)' is a near-restatement of the primary. Kritik covers both pointing out flaws and appreciative critique but these are the same English word; the distinction is too fine for a flashcard. Clear the redundant list.
Sub-definition 'to critique, to review (critically appraise)' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule. Fix phrasing.

Primary 'crown' is correct for the headwear/symbol sense. But 'krona' (Swedish currency) is a major everyday sense missing from the list. Sub-definition 'the Crown (state)' is correct but 'the' should not appear. Rebuild the list.

Current translation 'krona (Swedish currency)' includes a parenthetical that reads definition-like. The bare translation is 'krona'; parenthetical context belongs in the list. Also the list should note the crown/headwear sense that is missing.
Sub-definitions 'a body (more generally)' nearly restates the primary; 'a field' is marginal/technical. The core sense is simply 'body'. Clear the distracting list.
Current translation 'demand, to forcefully request' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule. Also 'to forcefully request' is a definition fragment. Fix to bare 'demand, require'.
Sub-definition 'fun things' is a noun usage, not an adj sense. For pos:adj the real senses are 'fun' and 'funny'. Drop 'fun things'.
Sub-definitions are padded — four entries including duplicates of the primary. Compress into distinct senses.
Sub-definition 'A component of certain games' is definition-like and vague. Rephrase as a clean short gloss.
Senses list mixes translations and definitions. Clean up to bare glosses with short parentheticals.
senses: know how to (a skill or language)

reason: Primary 'quality' in the 'level of excellence' sense is uncountable. 'A quality, a property' is a distinct countable sense. Mark primary as uncountable.
Current translation 'team, a workgroup' embeds 'a' — violates bare rule.
Sub-definitions include 'team (in sports)' which belongs to the other 'lag' entry (ett gender). This entry (en gender) is for law + brine. Remove the misplaced team sense.
Sub-definition 'legislation (the resulting laws)' is near-identical to the primary. Both senses are captured by 'legislation'. Single meaning — clear the list.
Current translation 'country, a land' embeds 'a' — violates bare rule.
Sub-definitions have two near-duplicate 'guide/direct' entries. Rationalise into distinct senses.

The parenthetical in the translation is helpful for this tricky word (ledamot is not the generic 'member'). Keep it, but simplify slightly. No senses list needed — the archaic 'body part/limb' sense is too marginal to include.
Sub-definition 'race leader' is too narrow — it's just 'leader (in a race)'. Rephrase the list cleanly.

Primary 'lead' is the sports/competition sense but 'ledning' in everyday Swedish most commonly means management/leadership of an organisation. Promote 'management, leadership' and keep 'lead' in the list.
Sub-definitions include 'a game, a pastime' and 'a game, playing' which overlap. Clean up and keep 'deck of cards' as real Swedish sense.
Sub-definition 'look throughout (a place) for something' is a definition, not a translation. The verb means 'search, look for' — single meaning.
Current translation 'live, to be alive' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule.
Sub-definition 'be in a horizontal position' is a definition of the primary sense 'lie', not a separate meaning. Fold it in. Remaining senses are distinct.
senses: lie (be horizontal or be located somewhere)

reason: Current translation 'feel, to sense' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule.
Primary 'love' (mass noun, strong affection) is uncountable. 'A love' (darling/sweetheart) is a distinct countable sense. Mark primary uncountable.
Current translation 'buy, to purchase' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule.
Sub-definitions include very marginal senses (sing in choir, backing vocalist). Drop those. Keep the four main everyday senses.
Current translation 'teach, to instruct' has 'to' embedded — violates bare rule. Also senses list needs cleaning.
Current translation 'lay, to put, to place' has 'to' embedded twice — violates bare rule. Core meaning is simple: lay, put, place.
Sub-definitions include 'to not take away with oneself but leave as available for others' which is a definition, not a translation. Clean the list.
'Apposite' is a rare formal word adding little value for learners. Drop it.
Sub-definitions list 'a link (in a chain)' as primary and 'a link (connection)' and 'a chain' as separate senses, but chain = many links, and these are overlapping. Rationalise.
Sub-definition 'to link (have or provide with a hyperlink)' restates the primary with 'to' embedded. Both senses of 'länka' are just 'link'. Single meaning — clear the list.
Current translation 'song, a tune, a track, a musical piece' embeds 'a' and is over-long. Shorten to 'song, tune, track'.

Primary 'make sound' is awkward phrasing. In Swedish 'låta' is most commonly used as 'sound' (låter bra = sounds good) and 'let, allow' (låt mig = let me). These are co-equal everyday senses; use semicolon form.
senses: sound (produce or seem a certain sound)

reason: Sub-definition 'an opportunity (from notion of an (implied good) situation)' is definition-like. Shorten it. Also primary 'position' alone is slightly underspecific — 'position, location' captures the main sense better.
Primary 'light' alone misses that 'lätt' very commonly means 'easy' in Swedish. These two meanings (light in weight, easy) are nearly co-equal for this word. Use semicolon form.

Sub-definition 'reward' should be in the list, and wiktionary lists it first (suggesting it may be the etymological primary), but in modern Swedish 'lön' overwhelmingly means salary/wage. Keep salary as primary, reward as secondary.
Sub-definitions include redundant near-duplicates and the wiktionary sense 'release, set free' is missing. Clean up.
senses: work out, resolve itself (of a problem)

reason: Sub-definition 'ruling majority (coalition in parliament or council)' is definition-like. Shorten it.

Sub-definition 'one, you (impersonal pronoun)' restates the primary as synonyms, not a different sense. But the primary 'people in general' alone doesn't show learners how to render it in English — adding 'one, you' to the primary is more helpful. Clear the sub-definition list.
Sub-definitions have near-duplicate entries. Rationalise into clean distinct senses.
Sub-definitions list four glosses all translating as 'market' in different contexts. Compress into three meaningful sense groupings.
senses: market (group of customers or economic area)

reason: Sub-definitions include pulp/paper manufacturing which is very marginal. The colloquial 'a lot of' sense ('en massa saker') is very common and should be kept. Rationalise.
Primary 'material' in the substance sense is uncountable. 'A matter, a subject' is a distinct countable sense. Mark primary uncountable.
Sub-definition 'during the same time that' is a definition of 'while', not a separate sense. Single meaning — clear the list.
recover pilot fix: archaic 'fief, fiefdom' was primary; the real everyday meaning is the administrative 'county'
